meanings.definition: a verbal act of admitting defeat
the act of conceding or yielding
be the cause or source of
examples: He gave me a lot of trouble
Our meeting afforded much interesting information

meanings.definition: end resistance, as under pressure or force
examples: The door yielded to repeated blows with a battering ram
The cow brings in 5 liters of milk
This year's crop yielded 1,000 bushels of corn
The estate renders some revenue for the family

meanings.definition: give over; surrender or relinquish to the physical control of another
give in, as to influence or pressure
move in order to make room for someone for something
examples: The park gave way to a supermarket

meanings.definition: cause to happen or be responsible for
examples: His two singles gave the team the victory
How much does this savings certificate pay annually?

meanings.definition: be flexible under stress of physical force
inclined to yield to argument or influence or control
lacking stiffness and giving way to pressure
examples: a deep yielding layer of foam rubber

meanings.definition: tending to give in or surrender or agree; - V.I.Parrington
examples: too yielding to make a stand against any encroachments
